scenarios.name: All Docker host-forwarded ports bind to 127.0.0.1

evidenceRefs.location: docker-compose.yml ports stanzas (postgres :11, nats :42-43, smackerel-core :76, smackerel-ml :137, ollama :174 — all literal 127.0.0.1: prefix)

linkedDoD: All ports: entries in docker-compose.yml use 127.0.0.1: prefix; SCN-020-001 DoD bullet in scopes.md Scope 1

scenarios.name: Config generation produces localhost-bound port mappings

evidenceRefs.location: internal/config/docker_security_test.go::TestDockerCompose_AllPortsBindLocalhost (in-process proxy: docker-compose.yml literal 127.0.0.1: prefixes match the SST host_bind_address value)

linkedDoD: scripts/commands/config.sh reads host_bind_address and writes HOST_BIND_ADDRESS to env; SCN-020-002 DoD bullet in scopes.md Scope 1

scenarios.name: NATS auth token is not visible in docker ps

evidenceRefs.location: docker-compose.yml NATS service uses --config /etc/nats/nats.conf instead of --auth

linkedDoD: NATS auth token is NOT visible in docker ps output; auth token now lives in mounted nats.conf (not in command: args)

scenarios.name: NATS uses config file for authentication

evidenceRefs.location: scripts/commands/config.sh generates config/generated/nats.conf with resolved token

linkedDoD: docker-compose.yml NATS service uses --config /etc/nats/nats.conf instead of --auth; mounts ./config/generated/nats.conf:/etc/nats/nats.conf:ro

scenarios.name: ML sidecar rejects unauthenticated requests when token configured

evidenceRefs.location: ml/app/auth.py::verify_auth raises HTTPException(401) when token missing or wrong

linkedDoD: ML sidecar non-health endpoints require auth when SMACKEREL_AUTH_TOKEN is set; SCN-020-005 DoD bullet in scopes.md Scope 2

scenarios.name: ML sidecar accepts authenticated requests

evidenceRefs.location: ml/app/auth.py::verify_auth uses hmac.compare_digest constant-time comparison and accepts both Authorization: Bearer and X-Auth-Token headers

linkedDoD: SCN-020-006 ML sidecar accepts authenticated requests DoD bullet in scopes.md Scope 2

scenarios.name: ML sidecar health endpoint remains unauthenticated

evidenceRefs.location: ml/app/main.py @app.get("/health") registered on app, not authed_router

linkedDoD: ML sidecar /health remains unauthenticated (Docker healthcheck works)

scenarios.name: ML sidecar allows all requests when auth_token is empty
linkedDoD: ML sidecar dev mode (empty token) allows all requests

scenarios.name: Web UI requires auth when auth_token is configured

evidenceRefs.location: internal/api/router.go:157 r.Use(deps.webAuthMiddleware) inside Web UI route group

linkedDoD: webAuthMiddleware applied to Web UI route group in internal/api/router.go; Web UI requires auth when auth_token configured

scenarios.name: Web UI allows all requests when auth_token is empty

evidenceRefs.location: internal/api/router.go webAuthMiddleware: if d.AuthToken == "" { next.ServeHTTP(w, r); return }

linkedDoD: Web UI passthrough when auth_token empty (dev mode)

scenarios.name: OAuth start endpoint is rate-limited

evidenceRefs.location: internal/api/router.go httprate.LimitByIP(10, 1*time.Minute) on OAuth start (and callback per SEC-SWEEP-001)

linkedDoD: httprate.LimitByIP(10, 1*time.Minute) applied to OAuth start endpoint

scenarios.name: OAuth start endpoint allows traffic within rate limit

evidenceRefs.location: internal/api/router.go httprate.LimitByIP allows ≤10 req/min per IP

linkedDoD: OAuth start ≤10 req/min per IP allowed

scenarios.name: Decryption fails closed when encryption key is configured

evidenceRefs.location: internal/auth/store.go::decrypt returns ("", error) on all 3 failure paths when encKey is non-nil; Get() propagates the error

linkedDoD: SCN-020-013 decrypt() returns ("", error) on all 3 failure paths when encKey is non-nil DoD bullet in scopes.md Scope 3

scenarios.name: No encryption key means plaintext passthrough

evidenceRefs.location: internal/auth/store.go::decrypt no-key passthrough branch: if len(s.encKey) == 0 { return encoded, nil }

linkedDoD: SCN-020-014 decrypt() returns (encoded, nil) when encKey is nil (dev mode passthrough) DoD bullet in scopes.md Scope 3

scenarios.name: Valid encrypted data decrypts successfully

evidenceRefs.location: internal/auth/store.go::decrypt AES-GCM Open path returns plaintext on success

linkedDoD: Valid encrypted data still decrypts correctly

scenarios.name: Startup emits warning when auth_token is empty (core)

linkedTests.function: (file exists; warn behavior verified by source pointer)

evidenceRefs.location: cmd/core/main.go:71 slog.Warn("SMACKEREL_AUTH_TOKEN is empty — system running without authentication") inside if cfg.AuthToken == "" branch

linkedDoD: cmd/core/main.go logs WARN on empty auth_token at startup

scenarios.name: ML sidecar emits warning when auth_token is empty

evidenceRefs.location: ml/app/main.py:55-57 lifespan logger.warning when auth_token is empty

linkedDoD: ml/app/main.py logs WARNING on empty SMACKEREL_AUTH_TOKEN at startup

scenarios.name: No warning when auth_token is configured

evidenceRefs.location: cmd/core/main.go and ml/app/main.py both gate the warning behind if token == ""

linkedDoD: No warning emitted when auth_token is configured (non-empty)
